retry_reason: kref_put decrements the refcount; if the refcount was already zero, that indicates a use-after-free bug; retrying would cause double-put, which is invalid.
contract_summary: batadv_neigh_ifinfo_put releases a reference on a batadv_neigh_ifinfo object. If the pointer is NULL, it's a no-op. After this call, the caller must not access the object anymore, as it may have been freed (if the reference count reached zero). This function is the release side of the reference-count life cycle; it must be paired with a previous get or creation that established a reference. The caller must guarantee that the object is not already being freed or that the refcount is not already zero (no double-put).
